defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser do
  @moduledoc """
  PDF parser. Prefers the PDF outline (bookmarks) for chapter splitting;
  falls back to text heading regex when the document has no outline.
  """

  @chapter_pattern ~r/^(Chapter\s+\d+[^\n]*|CHAPTER\s+\d+[^\n]*|Part\s+\d+[^\n]*)/m
  @outline_section_pattern ~r/<a name="outline"><\/a><h1>Document Outline<\/h1>\s*<ul>(.*?)<\/ul>/s
  @outline_entry_pattern ~r/<li><a href="[^#]*#(\d+)">([^<]+)<\/a>/

  def build_command(pdf_path, output_path) do
    {"pdftotext", ["-layout", pdf_path, output_path]}
  end

  def parse(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    metadata = read_metadata(pdf_path)

    chapters_result =
      case extract_outline(pdf_path) do
        {:ok, [_, _ | _] = entries} ->
          extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, metadata.page_count)

        _ ->
          extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path)
      end

    case chapters_result do
      {:ok, chapters} ->
        cover_image =
          case extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
            {:ok, bytes} -> bytes
            {:error, _} -> nil
          end

        {:ok,
         %{
           chapters: chapters,
           metadata: %{title: metadata.title, author: metadata.author},
           cover_image: cover_image
         }}

      {:error, _} = error ->
        error
    end
  end

  # Public for unit tests
  def parse_outline_entries(pdftohtml_html) do
    case Regex.run(@outline_section_pattern, pdftohtml_html, capture: :all_but_first) do
      [section] ->
        Regex.scan(@outline_entry_pattern, section, capture: :all_but_first)
        |> Enum.map(fn [page, title] ->
          %{title: html_decode(String.trim(title)), page: String.to_integer(page)}
        end)

      _ ->
        []
    end
  end

  def extract_chapters(text) do
    case Regex.split(@chapter_pattern, text, include_captures: true, trim: true) do
      parts when length(parts) >= 2 ->
        parts
        |> chunk_by_heading()
        |> Enum.with_index(1)
        |> Enum.map(fn {{title, content}, index} ->
          clean = String.trim(content)
          %{title: title, number: index, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}
        end)

      _ ->
        clean = String.trim(text)
        [%{title: "Full Text", number: 1, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}]
    end
  end

  defp extract_outline(pdf_path) do
    tmp_dir = Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_outline_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}")
    File.mkdir_p!(tmp_dir)
    stem = Path.join(tmp_dir, "doc")

    try do
      case System.cmd("pdftohtml", ["-i", "-nodrm", "-q", pdf_path, stem], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} ->
          html_path = stem <> "s.html"

          case File.read(html_path) do
            {:ok, html} -> {:ok, parse_outline_entries(html)}
            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read pdftohtml output: #{reason}"}
          end

        {output, code} ->
          {:error, "pdftohtml exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm_rf(tmp_dir)
    end
  end

  defp extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, page_count) do
    ranges =
      entries
      |> Enum.with_index()
      |> Enum.map(fn {entry, idx} ->
        start_page = entry.page

        end_page =
          case Enum.at(entries, idx + 1) do
            nil -> page_count
            next -> max(next.page - 1, start_page)
          end

        {entry.title, start_page, end_page}
      end)

    chapters =
      ranges
      |> Enum.with_index(1)
      |> Enum.reduce_while({:ok, []}, fn {{title, first, last}, number}, {:ok, acc} ->
        case extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
          {:ok, text} ->
            clean = String.trim(text)

            chapter = %{
              title: title,
              number: number,
              content: clean,
              word_count: word_count(clean)
            }

            {:cont, {:ok, [chapter | acc]}}

          {:error, _} = error ->
            {:halt, error}
        end
      end)

    case chapters do
      {:ok, list} -> {:ok, Enum.reverse(list)}
      error -> error
    end
  end

  defp extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path) do
    tmp_txt =
      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")

    try do
      {cmd, args} = build_command(pdf_path, tmp_txt)

      case System.cmd(cmd, args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} ->
          case File.read(tmp_txt) do
            {:ok, text} -> {:ok, extract_chapters(text)}
            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read extracted text: #{reason}"}
          end

        {output, code} ->
          {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm(tmp_txt)
    end
  end

  defp extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
    tmp_txt =
      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")

    try do
      args = ["-layout", "-f", "#{first}", "-l", "#{last}", pdf_path, tmp_txt]

      case System.cmd("pdftotext", args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} -> File.read(tmp_txt)
        {output, code} -> {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm(tmp_txt)
    end
  end

  defp read_metadata(pdf_path) do
    case System.cmd("pdfinfo", [pdf_path], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
      {output, 0} ->
        %{
          title: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Title") || pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path),
          author: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Author"),
          page_count: parse_pdfinfo_int(output, "Pages") || 1
        }

      _ ->
        %{title: pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path), author: nil, page_count: 1}
    end
  end

  defp parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
    case Regex.run(~r/^#{Regex.escape(name)}:\s+(.+)$/m, output) do
      [_, value] ->
        trimmed = String.trim(value)
        if trimmed == "", do: nil, else: trimmed

      _ ->
        nil
    end
  end

  defp parse_pdfinfo_int(output, name) do
    case parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
      nil -> nil
      value -> case Integer.parse(value) do
        {n, _} -> n
        _ -> nil
      end
    end
  end

  defp pdf_title_from_path(path), do: path |> Path.basename() |> Path.rootname()

  defp chunk_by_heading(parts) do
    parts
    |> Enum.reduce([], fn part, acc ->
      if Regex.match?(@chapter_pattern, String.trim(part)) do
        [{String.trim(part), ""} | acc]
      else
        case acc do
          [{title, existing} | rest] -> [{title, existing <> part} | rest]
          [] -> [{part, ""} | acc]
        end
      end
    end)
    |> Enum.reverse()
  end

  defp extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    File.mkdir_p!(storage_dir)
    output_prefix = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover")

    case System.cmd(
           "pdftoppm",
           [
             "-jpeg",
             "-f",
             "1",
             "-l",
             "1",
             "-scale-to-x",
             "300",
             "-scale-to-y",
             "400",
             pdf_path,
             output_prefix
           ],
           stderr_to_stdout: true
         ) do
      {_, 0} ->
        cover_file = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover-1.jpg")

        if File.exists?(cover_file) do
          bytes = File.read!(cover_file)
          File.rm(cover_file)
          {:ok, bytes}
        else
          {:error, :thumbnail_failed}
        end

      {output, _} ->
        {:error, "pdftoppm failed: #{output}"}
    end
  end

  defp word_count(text), do: text |> String.split(~r/\s+/, trim: true) |> length()

  defp html_decode(text) do
    text
    |> String.replace("&", "&")
    |> String.replace("<", "<")
    |> String.replace(">", ">")
    |> String.replace(""", "\"")
    |> String.replace("'", "'")
    |> String.replace("'", "'")
  end
end

defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser do
  @chapter_pattern ~r/^(Chapter\s+\d+[^\n]*|CHAPTER\s+\d+[^\n]*|Part\s+\d+[^\n]*)/m

  def build_command(pdf_path, output_path) do
    {"pdftotext", ["-layout", pdf_path, output_path]}
  end

  def parse(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    tmp_txt =
      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")

    try do
      {cmd, args} = build_command(pdf_path, tmp_txt)

      case System.cmd(cmd, args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_output, 0} ->
          case File.read(tmp_txt) do
            {:ok, text} ->
              chapters = extract_chapters(text)

              cover_image =
                case extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
                  {:ok, bytes} -> bytes
                  {:error, _} -> nil
                end

              {:ok,
               %{
                 chapters: chapters,
                 metadata: %{title: pdf_title(pdf_path), author: nil},
                 cover_image: cover_image
               }}

            {:error, reason} ->
              {:error, "Failed to read extracted text: #{reason}"}
          end

        {output, code} ->
          {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm(tmp_txt)
    end
  end

  def extract_chapters(text) do
    case Regex.split(@chapter_pattern, text, include_captures: true, trim: true) do
      parts when length(parts) >= 2 ->
        parts
        |> chunk_by_heading()
        |> Enum.with_index(1)
        |> Enum.map(fn {{title, content}, index} ->
          clean = String.trim(content)
          %{title: title, number: index, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}
        end)

      _ ->
        clean = String.trim(text)
        [%{title: "Full Text", number: 1, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}]
    end
  end

  defp chunk_by_heading(parts) do
    parts
    |> Enum.reduce([], fn part, acc ->
      if Regex.match?(@chapter_pattern, String.trim(part)) do
        [{String.trim(part), ""} | acc]
      else
        case acc do
          [{title, existing} | rest] -> [{title, existing <> part} | rest]
          [] -> [{part, ""} | acc]
        end
      end
    end)
    |> Enum.reverse()
  end

  defp extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    File.mkdir_p!(storage_dir)
    output_prefix = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover")

    case System.cmd(
           "pdftoppm",
           [
             "-jpeg",
             "-f",
             "1",
             "-l",
             "1",
             "-scale-to-x",
             "300",
             "-scale-to-y",
             "400",
             pdf_path,
             output_prefix
           ],
           stderr_to_stdout: true
         ) do
      {_, 0} ->
        # pdftoppm adds page number suffix: cover-1.jpg
        cover_file = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover-1.jpg")

        if File.exists?(cover_file) do
          bytes = File.read!(cover_file)
          File.rm(cover_file)
          {:ok, bytes}
        else
          {:error, :thumbnail_failed}
        end

      {output, _} ->
        {:error, "pdftoppm failed: #{output}"}
    end
  end

  defp word_count(text), do: text |> String.split(~r/\s+/, trim: true) |> length()
  defp pdf_title(path), do: path |> Path.basename() |> Path.rootname()
end

defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParserTest do use ExUnit.Case, async: true alias ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser describe "build_command/2" do test "constructs pdftotext command" do assert PdfParser.build_command("/tmp/test.pdf", "/tmp/output.txt") == {"pdftotext", ["-layout", "/tmp/test.pdf", "/tmp/output.txt"]} end end describe "extract_chapters/1" do test "splits text by chapter headings" do text = """ Chapter 1: The Beginning This is the first chapter content. Chapter 2: The Middle This is the second chapter. """ chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 2 assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Chapter 1: The Beginning" assert Enum.at(chapters, 1).title == "Chapter 2: The Middle" end test "handles CHAPTER uppercase headings" do text = "CHAPTER 1 Introduction\nSome text.\nCHAPTER 2 Methods\nMore text." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 2 end test "falls back to single chapter when no headings found" do text = "Some text without any chapter headings at all." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 1 assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Full Text" end test "counts words correctly" do text = "Chapter 1 Test\nOne two three four five." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).word_count == 5 end end describe "parse_outline_entries/1" do test "parses pdftohtml outline section into title/page entries" do html = """

Document Outline

  • INTRODUCTION
  • Chapter One: Aspects of Wholeness
  • Chapter Two: Another View of Evolution
""" entries = PdfParser.parse_outline_entries(html) assert length(entries) == 3 assert Enum.at(entries, 0) == %{title: "INTRODUCTION", page: 7} assert Enum.at(entries, 1).title == "Chapter One: Aspects of Wholeness" assert Enum.at(entries, 1).page == 10 assert Enum.at(entries, 2).page == 33 end test "decodes HTML entities in titles" do html = """

Document Outline

  • Vernadsky's & Holistic Science
""" [entry] = PdfParser.parse_outline_entries(html) assert entry.title == "Vernadsky's & Holistic Science" end test "returns empty list when no outline section present" do assert PdfParser.parse_outline_entries("no outline here") == [] end end end

""" chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 2 assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Chapter 1: The Beginning" assert Enum.at(chapters, 1).title == "Chapter 2: The Middle" end test "handles CHAPTER uppercase headings" do text = "CHAPTER 1 Introduction\nSome text.\nCHAPTER 2 Methods\nMore text." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 2 end test "falls back to single chapter when no headings found" do text = "Some text without any chapter headings at all." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert length(chapters) == 1 assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Full Text" end test "counts words correctly" do text = "Chapter 1 Test\nOne two three four five." chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text) assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).word_count == 5 end end describe "parse_outline_entries/1" do test "parses pdftohtml outline section into title/page entries" do html = """

Document Outline

  • INTRODUCTION
  • Chapter One: Aspects of Wholeness
  • Chapter Two: Another View of Evolution
""" entries = PdfParser.parse_outline_entries(html) assert length(entries) == 3 assert Enum.at(entries, 0) == %{title: "INTRODUCTION", page: 7} assert Enum.at(entries, 1).title == "Chapter One: Aspects of Wholeness" assert Enum.at(entries, 1).page == 10 assert Enum.at(entries, 2).page == 33 end test "decodes HTML entities in titles" do html = """

Document Outline

  • Vernadsky's & Holistic Science
""" [entry] = PdfParser.parse_outline_entries(html) assert entry.title == "Vernadsky's & Holistic Science" end test "returns empty list when no outline section present" do assert PdfParser.parse_outline_entries("no outline here") == [] end end end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParserTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser

  describe "build_command/2" do
    test "constructs pdftotext command" do
      assert PdfParser.build_command("/tmp/test.pdf", "/tmp/output.txt") ==
               {"pdftotext", ["-layout", "/tmp/test.pdf", "/tmp/output.txt"]}
    end
  end

  describe "extract_chapters/1" do
    test "splits text by chapter headings" do
      text = """
      Chapter 1: The Beginning

      This is the first chapter content.

      Chapter 2: The Middle

      This is the second chapter.
      """

      chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text)
      assert length(chapters) == 2
      assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Chapter 1: The Beginning"
      assert Enum.at(chapters, 1).title == "Chapter 2: The Middle"
    end

    test "handles CHAPTER uppercase headings" do
      text = "CHAPTER 1 Introduction\nSome text.\nCHAPTER 2 Methods\nMore text."
      chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text)
      assert length(chapters) == 2
    end

    test "falls back to single chapter when no headings found" do
      text = "Some text without any chapter headings at all."
      chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text)
      assert length(chapters) == 1
      assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).title == "Full Text"
    end

    test "counts words correctly" do
      text = "Chapter 1 Test\nOne two three four five."
      chapters = PdfParser.extract_chapters(text)
      assert Enum.at(chapters, 0).word_count == 5
    end
  end
end

  host = System.get_env("PHX_HOST", "localhost")

  config :readaloud_web, ReadaloudWebWeb.Endpoint,

    http: [ip: {0, 0, 0, 0}, port: port],
    secret_key_base: secret_key_base,
    server: true

  config :readaloud_tts,
    base_url: System.get_env("LOCALAI_URL", "http://localai:8080")

# This file is responsible for configuring your umbrella
# and **all applications** and their dependencies with the
# help of the Config module.
#
# Note that all applications in your umbrella share the
# same configuration and dependencies, which is why they
# all use the same configuration file. If you want different
# configurations or dependencies per app, it is best to
# move said applications out of the umbrella.
# This file is responsible for configuring your application
# and its dependencies with the aid of the Config module.
#
# This configuration file is loaded before any dependency and
# is restricted to this project.

# General application configuration

pdf = \"/mnt/bigmedia/media/Books/Raymond Peat/Generative Energy - Protecting and Restoring the Wholeness of Life/Raymond Peat - Generative Energy - Protecting and Restoring the Wholeness of Life.pdf\"
storage = \"/tmp/peat_test_storage\"

{:ok, result} = ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser.parse(pdf, storage)
IO.puts(\"title=#{result.metadata.title}\")
IO.puts(\"author=#{result.metadata.author}\")
IO.puts(\"chapters=#{length(result.chapters)}\")
IO.puts(\"cover_bytes=#{if result.cover_image, do: byte_size(result.cover_image), else: 0}\")
for ch <- result.chapters do
  IO.puts(\"  ##{ch.number}: #{ch.title} (#{ch.word_count} words)\")
end
" 2>&1 | tail -40'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser do
  @moduledoc """
  PDF parser. Prefers the PDF outline (bookmarks) for chapter splitting;
  falls back to text heading regex when the document has no outline.
  """

  @chapter_pattern ~r/^(Chapter\s+\d+[^\n]*|CHAPTER\s+\d+[^\n]*|Part\s+\d+[^\n]*)/m
  @outline_section_pattern ~r/<a name="outline"><\/a><h1>Document Outline<\/h1>\s*<ul>(.*?)<\/ul>/s
  @outline_entry_pattern ~r/<li><a href="[^#]*#(\d+)">([^<]+)<\/a>/

  def build_command(pdf_path, output_path) do
    {"pdftotext", ["-layout", pdf_path, output_path]}
  end

  def parse(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    metadata = read_metadata(pdf_path)

    chapters_result =
      case extract_outline(pdf_path) do
        {:ok, [_, _ | _] = entries} ->
          extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, metadata.page_count)

        _ ->
          extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path)
      end

    case chapters_result do
      {:ok, chapters} ->
        cover_image =
          case extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
            {:ok, bytes} -> bytes
            {:error, _} -> nil
          end

        {:ok,
         %{
           chapters: chapters,
           metadata: %{title: metadata.title, author: metadata.author},
           cover_image: cover_image
         }}

      {:error, _} = error ->
        error
    end
  end

  # Public for unit tests
  def parse_outline_entries(pdftohtml_html) do
    case Regex.run(@outline_section_pattern, pdftohtml_html, capture: :all_but_first) do
      [section] ->
        Regex.scan(@outline_entry_pattern, section, capture: :all_but_first)
        |> Enum.map(fn [page, title] ->
          %{title: html_decode(String.trim(title)), page: String.to_integer(page)}
        end)

      _ ->
        []
    end
  end

  def extract_chapters(text) do
    case Regex.split(@chapter_pattern, text, include_captures: true, trim: true) do
      parts when length(parts) >= 2 ->
        parts
        |> chunk_by_heading()
        |> Enum.with_index(1)
        |> Enum.map(fn {{title, content}, index} ->
          clean = String.trim(content)
          %{title: title, number: index, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}
        end)

      _ ->
        clean = String.trim(text)
        [%{title: "Full Text", number: 1, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}]
    end
  end

  defp extract_outline(pdf_path) do
    tmp_dir = Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_outline_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}")
    File.mkdir_p!(tmp_dir)
    stem = Path.join(tmp_dir, "doc")

    try do
      case System.cmd("pdftohtml", ["-i", "-nodrm", "-q", pdf_path, stem], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} ->
          html_path = stem <> "s.html"

          case File.read(html_path) do
            {:ok, html} -> {:ok, parse_outline_entries(html)}
            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read pdftohtml output: #{reason}"}
          end

        {output, code} ->
          {:error, "pdftohtml exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm_rf(tmp_dir)
    end
  end

  defp extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, page_count) do
    ranges =
      entries
      |> Enum.with_index()
      |> Enum.map(fn {entry, idx} ->
        start_page = entry.page

        end_page =
          case Enum.at(entries, idx + 1) do
            nil -> page_count
            next -> max(next.page - 1, start_page)
          end

        {entry.title, start_page, end_page}
      end)

    chapters =
      ranges
      |> Enum.with_index(1)
      |> Enum.reduce_while({:ok, []}, fn {{title, first, last}, number}, {:ok, acc} ->
        case extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
          {:ok, text} ->
            clean = String.trim(text)

            chapter = %{
              title: title,
              number: number,
              content: clean,
              word_count: word_count(clean)
            }

            {:cont, {:ok, [chapter | acc]}}

          {:error, _} = error ->
            {:halt, error}
        end
      end)

    case chapters do
      {:ok, list} -> {:ok, Enum.reverse(list)}
      error -> error
    end
  end

  defp extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path) do
    tmp_txt =
      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")

    try do
      {cmd, args} = build_command(pdf_path, tmp_txt)

      case System.cmd(cmd, args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} ->
          case File.read(tmp_txt) do
            {:ok, text} -> {:ok, extract_chapters(text)}
            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read extracted text: #{reason}"}
          end

        {output, code} ->
          {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm(tmp_txt)
    end
  end

  defp extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
    tmp_txt =
      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")

    try do
      args = ["-layout", "-f", "#{first}", "-l", "#{last}", pdf_path, tmp_txt]

      case System.cmd("pdftotext", args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
        {_, 0} -> File.read(tmp_txt)
        {output, code} -> {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
      end
    after
      File.rm(tmp_txt)
    end
  end

  defp read_metadata(pdf_path) do
    case System.cmd("pdfinfo", [pdf_path], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
      {output, 0} ->
        %{
          title: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Title") || pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path),
          author: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Author"),
          page_count: parse_pdfinfo_int(output, "Pages") || 1
        }

      _ ->
        %{title: pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path), author: nil, page_count: 1}
    end
  end

  defp parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
    case Regex.run(~r/^#{Regex.escape(name)}:\s+(.+)$/m, output) do
      [_, value] ->
        trimmed = String.trim(value)
        if trimmed == "", do: nil, else: trimmed

      _ ->
        nil
    end
  end

  defp parse_pdfinfo_int(output, name) do
    case parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
      nil -> nil
      value -> case Integer.parse(value) do
        {n, _} -> n
        _ -> nil
      end
    end
  end

  defp pdf_title_from_path(path), do: path |> Path.basename() |> Path.rootname()

  defp chunk_by_heading(parts) do
    parts
    |> Enum.reduce([], fn part, acc ->
      if Regex.match?(@chapter_pattern, String.trim(part)) do
        [{String.trim(part), ""} | acc]
      else
        case acc do
          [{title, existing} | rest] -> [{title, existing <> part} | rest]
          [] -> [{part, ""} | acc]
        end
      end
    end)
    |> Enum.reverse()
  end

  defp extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
    File.mkdir_p!(storage_dir)
    output_prefix = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover")

    case System.cmd(
           "pdftoppm",
           [
             "-jpeg",
             "-f",
             "1",
             "-l",
             "1",
             "-scale-to-x",
             "300",
             "-scale-to-y",
             "400",
             pdf_path,
             output_prefix
           ],
           stderr_to_stdout: true
         ) do
      {_, 0} ->
        cover_file = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover-1.jpg")

        if File.exists?(cover_file) do
          bytes = File.read!(cover_file)
          File.rm(cover_file)
          {:ok, bytes}
        else
          {:error, :thumbnail_failed}
        end

      {output, _} ->
        {:error, "pdftoppm failed: #{output}"}
    end
  end

  defp word_count(text), do: text |> String.split(~r/\s+/, trim: true) |> length()

  defp html_decode(text) do
    text
    |> String.replace("&", "&")
    |> String.replace("<", "<")
    |> String.replace(">", ">")
    |> String.replace(""", "\"")
    |> String.replace("'", "'")
    |> String.replace("'", "'")
  end
end

lines: +        # pdftoppm appends a page suffix that varies by version: "cover-1.jpg"
+        # on older releases, "cover-001.jpg" on newer ones.

1	defmodule ReadaloudImporter.PdfParser do
2	  @moduledoc """
3	  PDF parser. Prefers the PDF outline (bookmarks) for chapter splitting;
4	  falls back to text heading regex when the document has no outline.
5	  """
6	
7	  @chapter_pattern ~r/^(Chapter\s+\d+[^\n]*|CHAPTER\s+\d+[^\n]*|Part\s+\d+[^\n]*)/m
8	  @outline_section_pattern ~r/<a name="outline"><\/a><h1>Document Outline<\/h1>\s*<ul>(.*?)<\/ul>/s
9	  @outline_entry_pattern ~r/<li><a href="[^#]*#(\d+)">([^<]+)<\/a>/
10	
11	  def build_command(pdf_path, output_path) do
12	    {"pdftotext", ["-layout", pdf_path, output_path]}
13	  end
14	
15	  def parse(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
16	    metadata = read_metadata(pdf_path)
17	
18	    chapters_result =
19	      case extract_outline(pdf_path) do
20	        {:ok, [_, _ | _] = entries} ->
21	          extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, metadata.page_count)
22	
23	        _ ->
24	          extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path)
25	      end
26	
27	    case chapters_result do
28	      {:ok, chapters} ->
29	        cover_image =
30	          case extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
31	            {:ok, bytes} -> bytes
32	            {:error, _} -> nil
33	          end
34	
35	        {:ok,
36	         %{
37	           chapters: chapters,
38	           metadata: %{title: metadata.title, author: metadata.author},
39	           cover_image: cover_image
40	         }}
41	
42	      {:error, _} = error ->
43	        error
44	    end
45	  end
46	
47	  # Public for unit tests
48	  def parse_outline_entries(pdftohtml_html) do
49	    case Regex.run(@outline_section_pattern, pdftohtml_html, capture: :all_but_first) do
50	      [section] ->
51	        Regex.scan(@outline_entry_pattern, section, capture: :all_but_first)
52	        |> Enum.map(fn [page, title] ->
53	          %{title: html_decode(String.trim(title)), page: String.to_integer(page)}
54	        end)
55	
56	      _ ->
57	        []
58	    end
59	  end
60	
61	  def extract_chapters(text) do
62	    case Regex.split(@chapter_pattern, text, include_captures: true, trim: true) do
63	      parts when length(parts) >= 2 ->
64	        parts
65	        |> chunk_by_heading()
66	        |> Enum.with_index(1)
67	        |> Enum.map(fn {{title, content}, index} ->
68	          clean = String.trim(content)
69	          %{title: title, number: index, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}
70	        end)
71	
72	      _ ->
73	        clean = String.trim(text)
74	        [%{title: "Full Text", number: 1, content: clean, word_count: word_count(clean)}]
75	    end
76	  end
77	
78	  defp extract_outline(pdf_path) do
79	    tmp_dir =
80	      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_outline_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}")
81	
82	    File.mkdir_p!(tmp_dir)
83	    stem = Path.join(tmp_dir, "doc")
84	
85	    try do
86	      case System.cmd("pdftohtml", ["-i", "-nodrm", "-q", pdf_path, stem], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
87	        {_, 0} ->
88	          html_path = stem <> "s.html"
89	
90	          case File.read(html_path) do
91	            {:ok, html} -> {:ok, parse_outline_entries(html)}
92	            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read pdftohtml output: #{reason}"}
93	          end
94	
95	        {output, code} ->
96	          {:error, "pdftohtml exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
97	      end
98	    after
99	      File.rm_rf(tmp_dir)
100	    end
101	  end
102	
103	  defp extract_chapters_by_outline(pdf_path, entries, page_count) do
104	    ranges =
105	      entries
106	      |> Enum.with_index()
107	      |> Enum.map(fn {entry, idx} ->
108	        start_page = entry.page
109	
110	        end_page =
111	          case Enum.at(entries, idx + 1) do
112	            nil -> page_count
113	            next -> max(next.page - 1, start_page)
114	          end
115	
116	        {entry.title, start_page, end_page}
117	      end)
118	
119	    chapters =
120	      ranges
121	      |> Enum.with_index(1)
122	      |> Enum.reduce_while({:ok, []}, fn {{title, first, last}, number}, {:ok, acc} ->
123	        case extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
124	          {:ok, text} ->
125	            clean = String.trim(text)
126	
127	            chapter = %{
128	              title: title,
129	              number: number,
130	              content: clean,
131	              word_count: word_count(clean)
132	            }
133	
134	            {:cont, {:ok, [chapter | acc]}}
135	
136	          {:error, _} = error ->
137	            {:halt, error}
138	        end
139	      end)
140	
141	    case chapters do
142	      {:ok, list} -> {:ok, Enum.reverse(list)}
143	      error -> error
144	    end
145	  end
146	
147	  defp extract_chapters_by_regex(pdf_path) do
148	    tmp_txt =
149	      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")
150	
151	    try do
152	      {cmd, args} = build_command(pdf_path, tmp_txt)
153	
154	      case System.cmd(cmd, args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
155	        {_, 0} ->
156	          case File.read(tmp_txt) do
157	            {:ok, text} -> {:ok, extract_chapters(text)}
158	            {:error, reason} -> {:error, "Failed to read extracted text: #{reason}"}
159	          end
160	
161	        {output, code} ->
162	          {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
163	      end
164	    after
165	      File.rm(tmp_txt)
166	    end
167	  end
168	
169	  defp extract_text_for_pages(pdf_path, first, last) do
170	    tmp_txt =
171	      Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "readaloud_pdf_#{:erlang.unique_integer([:positive])}.txt")
172	
173	    try do
174	      args = ["-layout", "-f", "#{first}", "-l", "#{last}", pdf_path, tmp_txt]
175	
176	      case System.cmd("pdftotext", args, stderr_to_stdout: true) do
177	        {_, 0} -> File.read(tmp_txt)
178	        {output, code} -> {:error, "pdftotext exited with #{code}: #{output}"}
179	      end
180	    after
181	      File.rm(tmp_txt)
182	    end
183	  end
184	
185	  defp read_metadata(pdf_path) do
186	    case System.cmd("pdfinfo", [pdf_path], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
187	      {output, 0} ->
188	        %{
189	          title: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Title") || pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path),
190	          author: parse_pdfinfo_field(output, "Author"),
191	          page_count: parse_pdfinfo_int(output, "Pages") || 1
192	        }
193	
194	      _ ->
195	        %{title: pdf_title_from_path(pdf_path), author: nil, page_count: 1}
196	    end
197	  end
198	
199	  defp parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
200	    case Regex.run(~r/^#{Regex.escape(name)}:\s+(.+)$/m, output) do
201	      [_, value] ->
202	        trimmed = String.trim(value)
203	        if trimmed == "", do: nil, else: trimmed
204	
205	      _ ->
206	        nil
207	    end
208	  end
209	
210	  defp parse_pdfinfo_int(output, name) do
211	    case parse_pdfinfo_field(output, name) do
212	      nil ->
213	        nil
214	
215	      value ->
216	        case Integer.parse(value) do
217	          {n, _} -> n
218	          _ -> nil
219	        end
220	    end
221	  end
222	
223	  defp pdf_title_from_path(path), do: path |> Path.basename() |> Path.rootname()
224	
225	  defp chunk_by_heading(parts) do
226	    parts
227	    |> Enum.reduce([], fn part, acc ->
228	      if Regex.match?(@chapter_pattern, String.trim(part)) do
229	        [{String.trim(part), ""} | acc]
230	      else
231	        case acc do
232	          [{title, existing} | rest] -> [{title, existing <> part} | rest]
233	          [] -> [{part, ""} | acc]
234	        end
235	      end
236	    end)
237	    |> Enum.reverse()
238	  end
239	
240	  defp extract_thumbnail(pdf_path, storage_dir) do
241	    File.mkdir_p!(storage_dir)
242	    output_prefix = Path.join(storage_dir, "cover")
243	
244	    case System.cmd(
245	           "pdftoppm",
246	           [
247	             "-jpeg",
248	             "-f",
249	             "1",
250	             "-l",
251	             "1",
252	             "-scale-to-x",
253	             "300",
254	             "-scale-to-y",
255	             "400",
256	             pdf_path,
257	             output_prefix
258	           ],
259	           stderr_to_stdout: true
260	         ) do

Inspect PDF outline/structure to understand chapter layout
Add outline-based chapter extraction to PdfParser + use pdfinfo for metadata
Add tests for new parser logic
Run mix precommit, deploy via colmena
Re-import book and queue TTS with proper chapters

input.command:
cd ~/projects/readaloud && git add apps/readaloud_importer/lib/readaloud_importer/pdf_parser.ex apps/readaloud_importer/test/pdf_parser_test.exs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: split PDF chapters by outline bookmarks, read pdfinfo metadata

The PDF parser fell back to "Full Text" (one giant chapter) for any
document whose chapter headings didn't match the narrow regex
(e.g. "Chapter One:" instead of "Chapter 1"). Title was guessed from
the filename and author was always nil. Cover thumbnail also broke
silently with newer poppler (cover-001.jpg vs cover-1.jpg).

Now: prefer the PDF outline (bookmarks) via pdftohtml when present,
extracting per-chapter text with pdftotext page ranges. Read Title /
Author / Pages from pdfinfo. Match cover output by glob to handle
both poppler naming conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

file.content:
{ nixpkgs }:
let
  inherit (nixpkgs) gnugrep findutils;
in
nixpkgs.writeShellScriptBin "lint-grep" ''
  set -euo pipefail
  errors=0

  check_grep() {
    local pattern="$1" include="$2" msg="$3"
    local matches
    if matches=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -rn "$pattern" --include="$include" --exclude-dir=deps --exclude-dir=_build --exclude-dir=node_modules . 2>/dev/null); then
      echo "ERROR: $msg"
      echo "$matches"
      ((errors++)) || true
    fi
  }

  # no-flash-group-outside-layouts
  flash_matches=$(${findutils}/bin/find . -name '*.heex' ! -name '*layout*' ! -path '*/layouts/*' ! -path '*/deps/*' ! -path '*/_build/*' \
    -exec ${gnugrep}/bin/grep -ln '<\.flash_group' {} + 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$flash_matches" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: <.flash_group> must only be used inside layouts."
    echo "$flash_matches"
    ((errors++)) || true
  fi

  # no-form-for-changeset
  check_grep \
    '<\.form.*for={@changeset}' \
    '*.heex' \
    'Never pass @changeset to <.form>. Use to_form/2 to create @form first.'

  # no-form-let
  check_grep \
    '<\.form.*let={' \
    '*.heex' \
    '<.form let={f}> is deprecated. Use <.form for={@form}> and @form[:field].'

  # no-enum-each-in-heex
  check_grep \
    'Enum\.each' \
    '*.heex' \
    "Don't use Enum.each in HEEx. Use for comprehension: <%= for item <- @items do %>."

  # no-css-apply
  check_grep \
    '@apply ' \
    '*.css' \
    "Don't use @apply in CSS. Write Tailwind classes directly on elements."

  # no-is-prefix-functions
  is_matches=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -rEn '^\s*(def|defp)\s+is_' --include='*.ex' --include='*.exs' --exclude-dir=deps --exclude-dir=_build . 2>/dev/null \
    | ${gnugrep}/bin/grep -v 'defguard' || true)
  if [ -n "$is_matches" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Predicate functions should end with ? not start with is_. Reserve is_ for guards."
    echo "$is_matches"
    ((errors++)) || true
  fi

  # no-nested-modules
  while IFS= read -r file; do
    count=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -c '^defmodule ' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 1 ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Multiple top-level modules in $file — causes cyclic dependencies."
      ((errors++)) || true
    fi
  done < <(${findutils}/bin/find . -name '*.ex' -not -path './_build/*' -not -path './deps/*' 2>/dev/null)

  if [ "$errors" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Found $errors grep lint error(s)."
    exit 1
  fi
''

{ nixpkgs }:
let
  inherit (nixpkgs) gnugrep findutils;
in
nixpkgs.writeShellScriptBin "lint-grep" ''
  set -euo pipefail
  errors=0

  check_grep() {
    local pattern="$1" include="$2" msg="$3"
    local matches
    if matches=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -rn "$pattern" --include="$include" --exclude-dir=deps --exclude-dir=_build --exclude-dir=node_modules . 2>/dev/null); then
      echo "ERROR: $msg"
      echo "$matches"
      ((errors++)) || true
    fi
  }

  # no-flash-group-outside-layouts
  flash_matches=$(${findutils}/bin/find . -name '*.heex' ! -name '*layout*' ! -path '*/layouts/*' ! -path '*/deps/*' ! -path '*/_build/*' \
    -exec ${gnugrep}/bin/grep -ln '<\.flash_group' {} + 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$flash_matches" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: <.flash_group> must only be used inside layouts."
    echo "$flash_matches"
    ((errors++)) || true
  fi

  # no-form-for-changeset
  check_grep \
    '<\.form.*for={@changeset}' \
    '*.heex' \
    'Never pass @changeset to <.form>. Use to_form/2 to create @form first.'

  # no-form-let
  check_grep \
    '<\.form.*let={' \
    '*.heex' \
    '<.form let={f}> is deprecated. Use <.form for={@form}> and @form[:field].'

  # no-enum-each-in-heex
  check_grep \
    'Enum\.each' \
    '*.heex' \
    "Don't use Enum.each in HEEx. Use for comprehension: <%= for item <- @items do %>."

  # no-css-apply
  check_grep \
    '@apply ' \
    '*.css' \
    "Don't use @apply in CSS. Write Tailwind classes directly on elements."

  # no-is-prefix-functions
  is_matches=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -rEn '^\s*(def|defp)\s+is_' --include='*.ex' --include='*.exs' --exclude-dir=deps --exclude-dir=_build . 2>/dev/null \
    | ${gnugrep}/bin/grep -v 'defguard' || true)
  if [ -n "$is_matches" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Predicate functions should end with ? not start with is_. Reserve is_ for guards."
    echo "$is_matches"
    ((errors++)) || true
  fi

  # no-nested-modules
  while IFS= read -r file; do
    count=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -c '^defmodule ' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 1 ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Multiple top-level modules in $file — causes cyclic dependencies."
      ((errors++)) || true
    fi
  done < <(${findutils}/bin/find . -name '*.ex' -not -path './_build/*' -not -path './deps/*' 2>/dev/null)

  if [ "$errors" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Found $errors grep lint error(s)."
    exit 1
  fi
''

  while IFS= read -r file; do
    count=$(${gnugrep}/bin/grep -c '^defmodule ' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 1 ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Multiple top-level modules in $file — causes cyclic dependencies."
      ((errors++)) || true
    fi
  done < <(${findutils}/bin/find . -name '*.ex' -not -path './_build/*' -not -path './deps/*' 2>/dev/null)

  if [ "$errors" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Found $errors grep lint error(s)."
    exit 1
  fi
''

lines: echo "ERROR: Multiple top-level modules in $file — causes cyclic dependencies."

the filename and author was always nil. Cover thumbnail also broke

both poppler naming conventions.

worktree deps and producing noise) and quiet a stderr "integer
expected" message in the no-nested-modules check.

6	- [feedback_deploy_colmena.md](feedback_deploy_colmena.md) - Use colmena (not SSH + nixos-rebuild) to deploy NixOS changes to pylon
7	- [feedback_visual_companion_remote.md](feedback_visual_companion_remote.md) - Visual companion server: use 0.0.0.0 and hostname for remote SSH access
8	- [feedback_dual_memory.md](feedback_dual_memory.md) - Always write both file-based memories AND Honcho conclusions when saving
9	- [feedback_full_spec_paths.md](feedback_full_spec_paths.md) - Always use full absolute paths when referencing superpowers spec files
10	- [feedback_clean_solutions.md](feedback_clean_solutions.md) - Prefer clean architectural solutions over hacks/workarounds, even if more upfront work
11	- [project_readaloud_deployment.md](project_readaloud_deployment.md) - Readaloud deployed on pylon as native NixOS module via configs flake input
12	- [feedback_honcho_dreams.md](feedback_honcho_dreams.md) - Noah likes monitoring Honcho's meta synthesis (deductive/inductive observations) and wants visibility into derived patterns
13	- [project_ln_reader.md](project_ln_reader.md) - api-ln and web-ln are local user systemd services on phanteks, not pylon
14	- [project_ln_reader_migration_followup.md](project_ln_reader_migration_followup.md) - 4 ln-reader books skipped on 2026-05-01 migration; awaiting more downloads before re-run
15	- [project_hermes_shortform_v2.md](project_hermes_shortform_v2.md) - Hermes Short-Form Content Pipeline v2: narrator mode + ASCII quality floor
16	- [project_hermes_mindgame.md](project_hermes_mindgame.md) - Hermes mindgame skill: playable mind games as short-form video, spec at ~/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-17-hermes-mindgame-skill-design.md
17	- [project_nyx_deriver.md](project_nyx_deriver.md) - Nyx: fine-tuning Qwen3.5-0.8B to replace Honcho deriver with local model, spec at ~/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-18-nyx-deriver-finetuning-design.md
18	- [project_nyx_autoresearch_findings.md](project_nyx_autoresearch_findings.md) - Nyx autoresearch findings: optimal config, ceiling analysis, next directions for Qwen3.5-4B deriver training
19	- [project_nyx_handoff.md](project_nyx_handoff.md) - Handoff prompt for managing Nyx autoresearch subagents — current state, what worked, what to do next

22	- [project_ai_platform_consolidation.md](project_ai_platform_consolidation.md) - LiteLLM proxy consolidation: all AI through pylon:4100, CocoIndex RAG, Open WebUI, removing embed-dir/Ollama/Triton

Colmena 0.5.0-pre
Apply configurations on remote machines

Usage: colmena apply [OPTIONS] [GOAL]

Arguments:
  [GOAL]
          Deployment goal
          
          Same as the targets for switch-to-configuration, with the following extra pseudo-goals:
          
          - build: Only build the system profiles - push: Only copy the closures to remote nodes -
          keys: Only upload the keys to the remote nodes
          
          `switch` is the default goal unless `--reboot` is passed, in which case `boot` is the
          default.
          
          [default: switch]

          Possible values:
          - build:        Build the configurations only

content.text:
All done. Summary of what landed:

**Parser fix** (commit `c0b5d4e`, pushed to `ngngardner/readaloud` master, deployed to pylon via colmena):
- New flow: `pdftohtml` extracts the PDF outline, `pdftotext -f F -l L` pulls per-chapter text by page range. Falls back to the old regex when no outline is present.
- Title and author now come from `pdfinfo`, not the filename.
- Cover thumbnail glob fix for newer poppler (`cover-001.jpg` vs `cover-1.jpg`).
- `lint-grep` excludes `.claude/worktrees/` so checks don't trip on the stale Jason worktree's deps.

**Re-import:**
- Book id `19`, **23 chapters** (intro + 19 numbered + 3 PART headers), title "Generative Energy: Protecting and Restoring the Wholeness of Life", author "Raymond F. Peat".
- 23 TTS tasks queued with af_heart / kokoro. Currently 1 processing, 22 pending. Visible at `pylon:4000/books/19`.

The whole audiobook will take a while since each chapter chunks individually (Chapter One alone is ~5825 words). The reader will populate chapters as they finish.

content.content:
defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.AudioController do
  use ReadaloudWebWeb, :controller

  def stream(conn, %{"chapter_id" => chapter_id}) do
    case ReadaloudAudiobook.get_chapter_audio(String.to_integer(chapter_id)) do
      nil ->
        send_resp(conn, 404, "No audio")

      audio ->
        file_size = File.stat!(audio.audio_path).size

        conn =
          conn
          |> put_resp_content_type("audio/wav")
          |> put_resp_header("accept-ranges", "bytes")

        case get_req_header(conn, "range") do
          ["bytes=" <> range_spec] ->
            case parse_range(range_spec, file_size) do
              {range_start, range_end} ->
                length = range_end - range_start + 1

                conn
                |> put_resp_header(
                  "content-range",
                  "bytes #{range_start}-#{range_end}/#{file_size}"
                )
                |> send_file(206, audio.audio_path, range_start, length)

              :invalid ->
                conn
                |> put_resp_header("content-range", "bytes */#{file_size}")
                |> send_resp(416, "Range Not Satisfiable")
            end

          _ ->
            send_file(conn, 200, audio.audio_path)
        end
    end
  end

  defp parse_range(range_spec, file_size) do
    case String.split(range_spec, "-", parts: 2) do
      [start_str, ""] ->
        start = String.to_integer(start_str)
        if start < file_size, do: {start, file_size - 1}, else: :invalid

      ["", suffix_str] ->
        suffix = String.to_integer(suffix_str)
        start = max(0, file_size - suffix)
        {start, file_size - 1}

      [start_str, end_str] ->
        start = String.to_integer(start_str)
        end_pos = min(String.to_integer(end_str), file_size - 1)
        if start <= end_pos, do: {start, end_pos}, else: :invalid

      _ ->
        :invalid
    end
  end

  def timings(conn, %{"chapter_id" => chapter_id}) do
    case ReadaloudAudiobook.get_chapter_audio(String.to_integer(chapter_id)) do
      nil ->
        json(conn, %{timings: []})

      audio ->
        json(conn, %{timings: ReadaloudAudiobook.ChapterAudio.decoded_timings(audio)})
    end
  end

  def listen_redirect(conn, %{"id" => id, "chapter_id" => chapter_id}) do
    conn
    |> put_status(301)
    |> redirect(to: ~p"/books/#{id}/read/#{chapter_id}")
  end

  def cover(conn, %{"book_id" => book_id}) do
    book = ReadaloudLibrary.get_book!(book_id)

            ch = w.getnchannels()

import json, sys

"

file.content:
<%!-- State 3: Audio ready — full player --%>
      <div
        :if={@audio_state == :ready}
        id="audio-player"
        phx-hook="AudioPlayer"
        data-audio-url={~p"/api/books/#{@book.id}/chapters/#{@chapter.id}/audio"}
        data-timings-url={~p"/api/books/#{@book.id}/chapters/#{@chapter.id}/timings"}
        data-initial-position={@initial_position_ms}
        class="fixed bottom-0 inset-x-0 z-40 bg-base-200/95 backdrop-blur-xl border-t border-base-content/6 transition-all duration-300"
      >
        <audio id="audio-element" preload="auto"></audio>
        <div class="max-w-2xl mx-auto px-4 py-3 space-y-3">
          <%!-- Scrubber (hidden when collapsed) --%>
          <div
            data-scrubber
            class="w-full h-2 bg-base-300 rounded cursor-pointer relative select-none [.collapsed_&]:hidden"
          >
            <div
              data-progress-fill

3ec6821 chore(claude): migrate session hooks to user-level Nix config
4038bd5 feat: add mix task for retranscribing chapter audio timings
04f5428 fix: add TTS retry resilience for transient LocalAI errors
74f1af4 fix: validate cover image size and try larger covers first
---
commit 8b7b150947322a40a2afd274151e3696fca76cac
Author: Noah Gardner <ngngardner@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 13 17:11:35 2026 -0400

    feat: wire up missing UI features in reader template
    
    The feature/reader-ui-feedback branch merged JS hooks, CSS, and E2E
    tests but left the reader_live.ex template incomplete. This commit
    integrates the missing template elements:

content.content: Diagnose why audio.src isn't loading on chapter page
Add phx-update=ignore to audio element, commit, deploy

input.command:
cd ~/projects/readaloud && git add apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/reader_live.ex apps/readaloud_web/assets/js/hooks/audio_player.js && direnv exec . sh -c 'mix format apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/reader_live.ex apps/readaloud_web/assets/js/hooks/audio_player.js' 2>&1 | tail -3 && git diff --cached --stat && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(reader): keep audio src across LiveView re-patches; show duration before play

Two bugs in the reader's audio player:

1. After mounted() set <audio>.src = chapter URL, any subsequent LiveView
   patch of the parent template (e.g. when audio_state stayed :ready and the
   server pushed any other assign update) reset the src back to "" because
   the server-rendered <audio> tag has no src attribute. Result: readyState
   stuck at 0, browser never loaded metadata, time-display stuck at the
   template's default "0:00 / 0:00". phx-update=\"ignore\" tells LiveView
   to leave the element alone after first render.

2. The time-display was only refreshed inside the timeupdate listener, which
   doesn't fire until playback starts. The display showed \"0:00 / 0:00\"
   even when duration was already known, making it look like the audio
   hadn't loaded. Now refresh on loadedmetadata and durationchange too,
   and guard formatTime against NaN/Infinity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

cd ~/projects/readaloud && git add apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/reader_live.ex apps/readaloud_web/assets/js/hooks/audio_player.js && git diff --cached --stat && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(reader): keep audio src across LiveView re-patches; show duration before play

Two bugs in the reader's audio player:

1. After mounted() set <audio>.src = chapter URL, any subsequent LiveView
   patch of the parent template (e.g. when the server pushed any other
   assign update) reset the src back to "" because the server-rendered
   <audio> tag has no src attribute. Result: readyState stuck at 0, browser
   never loaded metadata, time-display stuck at the template's default
   "0:00 / 0:00". phx-update="ignore" tells LiveView to leave the element
   alone after first render.

2. The time-display was only refreshed inside the timeupdate listener,
   which doesn't fire until playback starts. The display showed "0:00 /
   0:00" even when duration was already known, making it look like the
   audio hadn't loaded. Now refresh on loadedmetadata and durationchange
   too, and guard formatTime against NaN/Infinity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
Repo back to its original WIP state with my fix committed and deployed.

**Summary:**

| | |
|---|---|
| Root cause | LiveView's DOM patcher reset `<audio>.src` to `""` on every re-patch (server template has no `src` attr). audio never loaded → readyState stuck at 0 → display stuck on the template default `0:00 / 0:00`. |
| Secondary bug | Even when duration *was* known, the time display wasn't updated until the first `timeupdate` event (which only fires during playback). |
| Fix | `phx-update="ignore"` on `<audio id="audio-element">` so LiveView leaves it alone after first render. Plus refresh time-display on `loadedmetadata`/`durationchange` and guard `formatTime` against NaN/Infinity. |
